sanskrit:
मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना।
मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः॥
transliteration:
mayā tatam idaṃ sarvaṃ jagad-avyakta-mūrtinā
mat-sthāni sarva-bhūtāni na cāhaṃ teṣv avasthitaḥ
literal_meaning: By Me, in unmanifest form, this entire universe is pervaded; all beings are in Me, yet I am not in them.
contextual_meaning: The Self (Ātman) is the silent, pervasive awareness that contains every thought, feeling, and perception, but is never contained by them; identifying with any content of mind is the root of anxiety.
psychological_state: Meta-cognitive awareness—observing the mind without fusing with its contents.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media, noticing the urge to 'become' the curated persona, then stepping back and watching the urge pass.
anchor_text: Rest as the space that holds every thought, never the thought itself.
